summary: Stellar 1969 performance with a superb soundboard capture, highlighted by a monster Viola Lee Blues and an epic Dark Star‑St Stephen‑Eleven‑Love Light sequence.
review: The recording is a clean SBD mix that lets the band’s raw 60s power shine – Jerry’s fuzzy tone, Phil’s thunderous bass and Bill & Mickey’s tight drumming are all front‑and‑center.  The show dazzles especially in the second set (Viola Lee Blues, Foxy Lady jam, Dark Star → St Stephen → The Eleven → Turn On Your Love Light) while the first set is a bit uneven but still solid.  Minor hiccups like a brief hiss, occasional cut‑outs and a missing few minutes of the Foxy Lady jam don’t detract from the overall brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (most 5‑star) praise both performance and soundboard quality; only a handful of minor complaints keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced SBD mix with excellent instrument separation. Reviewers note a slight hiss, occasional reel cut‑outs and a missing 3–4 min segment of the Foxy Lady jam.

standout_songs: Dark Star > St. Stephen > The Eleven > Turn On Your Love Light
poor_songs: Hard to Handle (some reviewers found the jam sloppy)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is a bit rough but builds momentum; second set erupts with sustained jams and tight transitions, making the concert feel like a continuous high‑energy journey.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone described as rich, fuzzy and “diggin’ in”; praised for improvisational fire, though his slide on Hard to Handle drew criticism.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass highlighted as huge, visceral and never lost in the mix; several reviewers call Phil’s playing a standout feature.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart praised for solid groove and dynamic duets; snare work on St Stephen noted as “firecracker‑like”.
